title: First jurisdiction overlay (credit reporting / account types / consumer rights)
context: The jurisdiction-neutral core does not cover country-specific money systems. A jurisdiction overlay adds clearly-labeled, locally-reviewed country-specific notes on topics like credit reporting, account types, and consumer-protection rights for a single jurisdiction — chosen by the criteria defined in the scope-decision document (task 005). The overlay is strictly additive and labeled, never presented as universal. It requires a local credentialed reviewer for that jurisdiction and full source vetting for jurisdiction-specific sources. The overlay jurisdiction is TO BE SECURED pending a local reviewer.
objective: Research, source-vet, draft, and get local-expert sign-off on the first jurisdiction overlay covering credit reporting, account types, and consumer-protection rights for the selected jurisdiction, as a labeled, clearly-additive overlay to the neutral core.
acceptanceCriteria: Overlay jurisdiction is chosen by the four criteria from task 005 and approved by the maintainer; specific jurisdiction is TO BE SECURED pending a local credentialed reviewer
Overlay covers credit reporting, basic account types, and consumer-protection rights for the specific jurisdiction — clearly labeled as jurisdiction-specific, never presented as universal
All sources are authoritative, openly-licensed, and jurisdiction-appropriate (e.g., national consumer-finance agency, central bank, nonprofit); reuse terms verified and recorded per source
Linter configured to allow jurisdiction-specific terms only inside labeled overlay sections; linter-clean across the overlay
Local credentialed reviewer (financial educator or consumer-finance professional for that jurisdiction) sign-off recorded for the shipped version
Overlay data conforms to the content JSON Schema with jurisdiction set to the ISO country code; CC-BY-SA inheritance handled if applicable
output: A reviewed, locally-expert-signed-off jurisdiction overlay dataset (data/overlays/<jurisdiction-code>/) with source records, labeled overlay content, and linter configuration — additive to the neutral core.
